violations.text: An employee was observed rinsing fruit in the Fruit Prep Room hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Proper use of hand-washing sink was discussed with the employee at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2026

The following chemicals were observed improperly stored... - Spray sanitizer was observed stored above food cans, food prep equipment, and single service items. - Sink dish washing and sanitizing chemicals were observed stored above the 2-bay fruit wash prep sink Chemicals must be stored in a way to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. (Spray bottle was relocated for proper storage at the time of inspection, and a technician arrived at time of inspection for plan to move chemicals mounted over the food prep sink.) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the ingredient food storage containers in the Prepared Foods Kitchen were observed to have an accumulation of food and old sticker/tape residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment are to be cleaned at a frequency necessary to keep them clean and to preclude the buildup of debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2026

The flow pressure of the fresh hot water sanitizing rinse (PSI) was not between 15-25 PSI as required (observed at 0-2 PSI). Based on use of thermo-labels, proper sanitizing was not achieved as evidenced by the thermo-label not turning black. Proper sanitization is to occur in the 3 Bay Sink until unit is adjusted or repaired. (Maintenance on site at time of inspection.) New Violation. Correct By: 12/17/2024

Chicken sandwiches in the heated-to-go hot hold shelving unit were observed held at 117-125 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Based on temperature and time of preparation, sandwiches were pulled for cooling. (Per the PIC, chicken sandwiches will no longer be held on the hot hold shelving until better solution devised.) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/17/2024

Scoops and spoons used for dispensing food items were observed stored in the food with the handles touching the food item. Utensils used for dispensing of food items are to be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Utensils were adjusted at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/17/2024

At the time of inspection, the Deli "Back Bar" cold hold unit was observed to be in poor repair (spots of rust/chipping finish). Equipment is to be maintained in good repair and/or replaced as needed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025

At the time of inspection, the following areas of plumbing were observed to be in need of repair/adjustment... - The Pre-Flush Sprayer at the 3 bay sink in the Pizza area was observed to be leaking from the sprayer hose attachment - The drainpipe to the 3 bay sink in the Meat Dept. area was observed to be dripping onto the floor Plumbing is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

Chicken nuggets were observed held at 118-122 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was reheated. Establishment stated that procedures will be reevaluated to ensure that food maintains a temperature above 135. Establishment will monitor food temperatures to ensure new policy is adequate. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2023

The ice scoop was observed stored in stagnant water with debris. Ice scoop was removed and sanitized. Per PIC, ice scoop holder will be installed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2023
The grout in the dishwasher area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Pooling water was observed between tiles. New Violation. Correct By: 11/11/2023
